Meta.titleDefault: PSOVA — Pakistan Society Of Vascular Anomaly
Meta.description: Pakistan's first society for vascular anomalies. Plain-language guidance, a nationwide specialist directory, and a community for families. Common. Treatable. You are not alone.

Common.placeholderNotice: Representative placeholder content — to be finalised by PSOVA.

Home.heroLead: Vascular anomalies are common and treatable. PSOVA connects families and specialists across Pakistan — so no one has to face a diagnosis alone.
Home.dualEyebrow: Where do you want to begin?
Home.patientsCardBody: Plain-language answers, what treatment looks like, and where to find care near you.
Home.prosCardBody: Membership, referral pathways, the ISSVA classification, research, and events.
Home.statsLead: Vascular anomalies are more common than most families realise — and the vast majority are manageable with the right team.
Home.missionBody: Until now, Pakistan had no dedicated society for vascular anomalies. Too many families search blindly, present late, or never reach the right team. PSOVA exists to change that — with trusted information, a verified care directory, and a community that understands.
Home.conditionsLead: From the common strawberry birthmark to rarer syndromes — clear, reassuring explanations grounded in the global ISSVA classification.
Home.mapLead: A growing, verified directory of centres and specialists — across every province.
Home.ctaBody: Whether you are a worried parent or a clinician who wants to help — there is a place for you at PSOVA.

About.title: A first for Pakistan
About.lead: The Pakistan Society Of Vascular Anomaly is a national, non-profit community connecting families, specialists, and centres to make expert care reachable everywhere.
About.missionBody: To raise awareness, end stigma, and ensure every person with a vascular anomaly in Pakistan can find timely, expert, multidisciplinary care.
About.visionBody: A Pakistan where a vascular anomaly diagnosis is met with clarity and compassion — not confusion, delay, or shame.
About.storyBody: Care exists in a handful of centres, but families across the country struggle to reach it. There has been no national voice — until now. PSOVA brings that voice, and a map to the help that already exists.
About.teamLead: A multidisciplinary board of clinicians, researchers, and family advocates.
About.partnersLead: We align our information with global standards and work alongside leading Pakistani institutions.

WhatAreVA.title: What are vascular anomalies?
WhatAreVA.lead: Conditions in which blood or lymphatic vessels form or grow abnormally. They are not contagious, not cancerous, and not caused by anything a parent did.
WhatAreVA.defBody: "Vascular" means blood vessels; an "anomaly" means something developed differently from usual. They can appear as a mark or lump on the skin, or lie deeper in muscle, bone, or organs. Most appear at or shortly after birth and range from harmless birthmarks to conditions needing specialist care.
WhatAreVA.tumorsBody: Growths made of cells that multiply — like infantile hemangioma. They often appear after birth, grow, then shrink.
WhatAreVA.malformationsBody: Vessels that formed incorrectly but do not multiply — like a port-wine stain. Usually present from birth and persistent, growing with the child.
WhatAreVA.classificationLead: The globally accepted system doctors use to name and group these conditions, so a patient gets a consistent diagnosis anywhere in the world.
WhatAreVA.keyPointBody: A tumour tends to appear, grow, then shrink. A malformation is usually there from birth, does not go away on its own, and grows in proportion with the child.
WhatAreVA.ctaBody: Clear, reassuring guides to the most common vascular anomalies.

Conditions.title: Understand the conditions
Conditions.lead: Plain-language, clinician-reviewed guides — grounded in the ISSVA classification.
Conditions.explorerLead: Tap a glowing point to see which conditions can appear where.
Conditions.explorerHint: Tap a point on the figure

Patients.title: You have found the right place
Patients.lead: A new diagnosis can be frightening. Here is what it means, what helps, and how to find care.
Patients.step1Body: The vast majority of vascular anomalies are not cancerous and are manageable.
Patients.step2Body: An experienced specialist can identify the exact type and what (if anything) to do.
Patients.step3Body: The best care brings several specialists together. Use our directory to find one.
Patients.treatmentBody: There is no single treatment — care is matched to the type, size, and location. Options include active monitoring, medicines (such as propranolol or sirolimus), laser, sclerotherapy, embolization, and surgery — often combined.
Patients.askLead: Have a question about a birthmark or diagnosis? Send it to our panel and we'll point you in the right direction.
Patients.supportBody: You are not alone. Connecting with families on the same journey changes everything — from isolation to understanding.
Patients.costBody: Much care in Pakistan is paid out of pocket. We share guidance on hospital zakat/charity funds and what programmes like Sehat Sahulat may cover.
Patients.schoolBody: Practical tips for school, sport, clothing, and answering questions with confidence.
Patients.stigmaBody: Birthmarks are not anyone's fault and not contagious. Compassion and accurate information protect a child's confidence.

Professionals.title: Join Pakistan's vascular anomaly network
Professionals.lead: A multidisciplinary community advancing standards, referral, research, and education.
Professionals.membershipBody: Open to dermatologists, paediatricians, interventional radiologists, surgeons, geneticists, and allied professionals committed to better vascular anomaly care.
Professionals.referralBody: Guidance on when and where to refer — toward experienced, multidisciplinary centres.
Professionals.classificationBody: We align with the international standard so diagnoses are consistent nationwide.
Professionals.researchBody: Collaborate on a planned national registry and multi-centre studies.
Professionals.eventsBody: Webinars, debates, and an annual meeting for the community.
Professionals.directoryBody: Help families find you — request a listing in our verified specialist directory.

Directory.title: Find care across Pakistan
Directory.lead: A growing, verified directory of centres and specialists treating vascular anomalies.
Directory.noResults: No centres match your filters yet. Try widening your search.
Directory.mapHint: Tap a city to filter the list
Directory.verifyNotice: This directory is representative and being verified. Always confirm details with the institution before travelling.

Research.title: Building the evidence for Pakistan
Research.lead: Better data means better care. PSOVA supports research that reflects our own patients.
Research.registryBody: A planned registry to map need, guide research, and improve care pathways nationwide.
Research.pubsBody: A curated list of Pakistani and international vascular anomaly research (coming soon).
Research.collabBody: Researchers and centres are invited to partner on multi-centre studies.

News.title: What's happening at PSOVA
News.lead: Announcements, awareness campaigns, events, and family stories.

GetInvolved.title: Help us reach every family
GetInvolved.lead: Your support builds awareness, the directory, and a community that no family in Pakistan has had before.
GetInvolved.donateBody: Fund awareness campaigns, the specialist directory, and family support.
GetInvolved.volunteerBody: Offer your time and skills — from translation to events to outreach.
GetInvolved.fundraiseBody: Run a campaign in your school, workplace, or community.
GetInvolved.partnerBody: Hospitals, NGOs, and companies — let's expand access together.
GetInvolved.awarenessBody: Share that vascular anomalies are common, treatable, and no one's fault.

Donate.title: Help every family reach
Donate.lead: Your gift funds awareness, Pakistan's first vascular-anomaly care directory, and direct support for children and families who have nowhere else to turn.
Donate.metaDescription: Support PSOVA — Pakistan's first vascular anomaly society. Give via bank transfer, JazzCash, Easypaisa, Raast or Zakat, sponsor a child's treatment, or pledge a monthly gift.
Donate.whyTitle: Your support changes a child's story
Donate.whyLead: Vascular anomalies are common and treatable — but too many families in Pakistan face them alone, late, or without the right team.
Donate.why1Body: We build a verified, nationwide directory so no family has to search blindly for help.
Donate.why2Body: Most vascular anomalies appear at or soon after birth. Early, expert care protects a child's health and confidence.
Donate.why3Body: Until now there was no national voice for these conditions. Your gift builds it.
Donate.tiersTitle: Every gift goes further than you think
Donate.tiersLead: Pick an amount to see the difference it makes — or give whatever you can.
Donate.tierCustomNote: Whatever you can give helps a family. Tell us your amount in the form below.
Donate.designationsTitle: Choose how your gift is used
Donate.designationsLead: Give as Zakat, a general Sadaqah, sponsor a child, or become a monthly supporter.
Donate.methodsTitle: Pick the method that suits you
Donate.methodsLead: Use any of the methods below, then send your receipt to us so we can thank you and confirm.
Donate.placeholderNote: Account details are being finalised — please call or WhatsApp us to confirm before transferring.
Donate.intlNote: Donating from outside Pakistan? Contact us for the latest international transfer details and currency options (USD, GBP, EUR).
Donate.cardComingSoonNote: Secure online card donations are on the way. For now, please use a method above or contact us directly.
Donate.trustLead: We are committed to handling every rupee with care and accountability.
Donate.trust1Body: Donations fund awareness, the care directory, and direct family support — not overheads first.
Donate.trust2Body: A volunteer board oversees our work, and we report on our activities openly.
Donate.trust3Body: Ask us for a receipt for any donation, including Zakat contributions.
Donate.useOfFunds1: Awareness campaigns that reach families before a diagnosis becomes a crisis.
Donate.useOfFunds2: Building and verifying the nationwide specialist directory.
Donate.useOfFunds3: Direct support toward diagnosis and treatment for children in need.
Donate.faqZakatA: Yes. When you choose Zakat, your donation is distributed only to eligible (mustahiqeen) patients for their treatment, in line with Shariah.
Donate.faqWhereA: Your gift funds awareness, the nationwide care directory, and direct support for patients and families.
Donate.faqReceiptA: Absolutely. Request a receipt for any donation — including Zakat — and we'll provide one.
Donate.faqMonthlyA: Yes. Choose 'Monthly giving' in the form and we'll set it up with you. You can pause or cancel anytime.
Donate.faqIntlQ: I live abroad — can I still donate?
Donate.faqIntlA: Yes. See the international section above, or contact us for the latest overseas transfer details and currency options.
Donate.faqSecureA: We never ask for your card PIN or password. Transfer directly to our listed accounts, and confirm details with us by phone or WhatsApp if in doubt.
Donate.faqConfirmQ: I've transferred — how do I confirm?
Donate.faqConfirmA: Please share your transfer receipt and your chosen designation by calling or messaging us at
Donate.formTitle: Make a pledge or request a call back
Donate.formLead: Tell us how you'd like to give and we'll guide you — or just leave your number and we'll call you.
Donate.formSuccess: Thank you! Your pledge has been received — we'll be in touch. To complete a transfer now, call or WhatsApp us below.
Donate.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again, or call/WhatsApp us directly.
Donate.formContactRequired: Please give us a phone number or email so we can reach you.
Donate.contactTitle: Prefer to talk to a person?
Donate.contactLead: Reach our donations lead directly — for confirmations, Zakat questions, or anything at all.
Donate.waPrefill: Assalam o Alaikum, I would like to donate to PSOVA.
Donate.finalTitle: Be the reason a family isn't alone
Donate.finalBody: Your generosity reaches a child who needs care, and a parent who needs hope. Give today.

Contact.title: Get in touch
Contact.lead: Questions, partnership, or media — we'd love to hear from you.
Contact.formSuccess: Thank you — your message has been received. We'll be in touch.
Contact.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly.

AskExpert.title: Ask the expert
AskExpert.lead: Send a question about a birthmark or diagnosis. This is general guidance, not a medical consultation.
AskExpert.name: Your name
AskExpert.question: Your question
AskExpert.consent: I understand this is general information, not a diagnosis or medical advice.
AskExpert.success: Thank you — your question has been received.

Footer.tagline: Common. Treatable. You are not alone.
Footer.newsletterLead: Awareness, events, and updates — occasionally, never spam.
Footer.builtNote: A non-profit initiative. Placeholder content for review.

Disclaimer.short: General information only — not a substitute for professional medical advice. Always consult a qualified specialist.
Disclaimer.full: The information on this site is provided for general educational purposes and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Content is being reviewed by clinicians. Always seek the advice of a qualified specialist for any medical concern.
